_comment: Illustrative LLM output. NPS stats are deterministic. Theme analysis will vary.

representative_quotes: I spent two days trying to understand how to set up projects correctly and gave up twice
The learning curve is too steep for non-technical folks
The recurring tasks feature saved us probably 2 hours per week
The recurring tasks alone paid for the subscription
Please add Microsoft Teams integration. Our company is all-in on Teams and Slack is not an option
The search function is terrible. I cannot find tasks from 3 months ago without scrolling through everything
About half the people still use a spreadsheet
Too many notifications and no way to silence specific projects
The only thing I miss is a Gantt view
Would love bulk editing of tasks
The dashboard takes about 4 seconds to load when I have many projects

top_3_insights: Onboarding is the #1 detractor driver — multiple users describe multi-day confusion before achieving first value. Improving the first-run experience could directly rescue low-NPS users.
Recurring tasks is the standout retention and value driver — two independent promoters attribute subscription justification to this single feature. Protect and promote it.
Team adoption is stalling for mid-size teams (10-15 people) — non-technical teammates are falling back to spreadsheets. This is a growth ceiling issue, not a power-user issue.
critical_issues: Onboarding confusion causing churn-risk: 2 users describe giving up during setup (R002, R006) — address with guided setup flow or contextual in-app help
Search is unusable for historical tasks (R012) — basic recall failure for a productivity tool
No Microsoft Teams integration blocking enterprise deals (R007) — already on roadmap
delight_signals: Recurring tasks feature has clear, quantified value ('saved 2 hours per week') — use in marketing copy
Task dependencies feature prevents missed handoffs — underrated, should be more discoverable
Support team rated very positively — fast resolution builds trust

feature_requests.request: Lower pricing tier for small teams

recommended_actions.action: Redesign onboarding flow with a guided project setup wizard
recommended_actions.rationale: Directly addresses the #1 detractor driver. Multiple users describe multi-day confusion. Even a 20% improvement in time-to-first-value would materially lift NPS.

recommended_actions.action: Fix search — prioritise historical task recall by date/project
recommended_actions.rationale: R012 is a detractor specifically because of search. A productivity tool with broken recall erodes trust.
Teams with 10+ members are overwhelmed by notifications. This is a natural growth pain that will worsen as team size increases.

recommended_actions.action: Promote recurring tasks more prominently in onboarding
recommended_actions.rationale: Two promoters explicitly attribute product value to this feature. New users likely don't discover it. Surface it in the setup wizard.
